steps.title: Connect slope to units
steps.content:
The slope of a graph is the change in $y$ divided by the change in $x$. Here, that means dollars per game.
$$\frac{\text{change in total cost}}{\text{change in games}}$$

steps.title: Separate slope and intercept
steps.content: The video game system's cost is the fixed starting cost, which is the $y$-intercept. The slope represents the added cost for each additional game.

steps.title: Choose the slope interpretation
steps.content: The interpretation that describes the cost of each game is the slope interpretation. The answer is A.
